transcript:
[+] This audio recording is intended for use with various automatic speech recognition services to generate machine transcripts. \\||


[+] We will submit the same audio to multiple services to obtain word-level TIME-CODES indicating when each word in the recording begins and ends. \\

[+] By comparing the word-accuracy and TIME-CODE-accuracy provided by each service, we can analyze the performance of each service on a per-word basis. \\||


[+] Since the spoken words in the source audio submitted to all A.S.R. services are consistent in wording and timing, we can directly compare how each service formats their word-TIME-CODE data. \\

[+] Understanding the structure of each timed-text file format will allow us to develop a converter that translates between them. \\||


[+] Initially, we will compare formats from YOU-TUBES Automatic Captions, DEEP-GRAMS Nova, Assembly A.EYES format, GENTLE-IS format, and a variant of OPEN A.EYES Whisper (WHISPER-X). \\

[+] We may also consider formats from ODDER A.I., HAPPY-S-TRIBE, and DIS-SCRIPT, although these are paid services and may not be included immediately. \\||


[+] Our goal is to create a SUPER-SET timed-text format that incorporates all features from the individual formats. \\

[+] This includes translating exclusive features of each format to the syntax of others, even if the original tools for each source format cannot read the extra features by default. \\

[+] For example, Whisper, DEEP-GRAM, and Assembly A.I. provide a confidence score for each word, while Gentle and YOU-TUBE do not. \\

[+] Conversely, Gentle offers per-PHONE-KNEE-HIM timing data, whereas others only provide per-word TIME-CODES. \\||


[+] Thank you for your attention. \\

[+] We hope this data will shed light on the features of each format. \\||
